title: Natural Language Processing
overview: NLP is AI understanding, processing, and generating human language.

blocks.title: What is NLP & Tokenization
blocks.body: NLP is AI understanding, processing, and generating human language.
bullets: NLP = Natural Language Processing — AI working with human language
Why language is hard for AI — same word can mean very different things depending on context
Tokenization — splitting text into individual words or parts AI can process
Bag of Words — representing text by counting how often each word appears

leftPanel.label: Real Life Analogy
leftPanel.title: Puzzle pieces
leftPanel.description: Breaking a sentence into puzzle pieces before solving it

blocks.question: Is tokenization the process of splitting text into smaller pieces AI can process?

blocks.title: Word Embeddings & Large Language Models
blocks.body: Word embeddings represent words as numbers. LLMs are very large neural networks trained on massive text.
bullets: Word Embeddings — words represented as numbers so AI can find similarities
Word math: King - Man + Woman = Queen — AI can do this kind of reasoning
Language Models predict the next word given the previous words
LLMs (Large Language Models) — trained on massive text data (GPT, Gemini, Claude)
Transformers — the architecture behind modern LLMs — processes all words at once

leftPanel.label: Real Life Analogy
leftPanel.title: Extremely well-read person
leftPanel.description: who has read the entire internet

centerPanel.label: Programming Concept
centerPanel.textContent: Words → Numbers (vectors) → Similar words are close together on a map

blocks.question: Do LLMs like ChatGPT predict the next word based on all the words before it?
blocks.answer: Yes, they predict the next most likely token based on their context and training.

blocks.title: NLP Steps
blocks.body: How text is processed by LLMs.
bullets: Step 1: Take raw text input from user
Step 2: Tokenize — split into words or subwords
Step 3: Convert tokens to numbers (word embeddings)
Step 5: Model predicts next token → generates output text

blocks.title: Practice Exercise
blocks.instructions: Ask ChatGPT the same question 3 different ways — write down how the answer changes each time
blocks.hint: See how minor wording changes affect the output.

keyTakeaways.text: NLP lets AI understand and generate human language
Tokenization splits text into pieces AI can process
Bag of Words counts word frequency to represent text
Word embeddings place words in number space — similar words are close together
LLMs are trained on massive text and predict the next word
Transformers process all words at once — powering GPT, Gemini, Claude

whatYouLearned: What NLP is and how it processes language
